title: Rich Crispy Japanese Rice Bowl
description: A comforting, crispy rice bowl with a rich soymirin glaze, bluecolored tofu topping, and a satisfying crunch, all eggfree.
instructions: Combine soy sauce, mirin, sake, sugar, and blue food coloring in a small bowl; stir until sugar dissolves to create the glaze.
Heat 1 tbsp sesame oil in a non‑stick skillet over medium‑high heat; add cubed tofu and fry until all sides are golden and crispy, about 5–6 minutes.
Add the glaze to the skillet, reduce heat to medium, and toss tofu until coated and the sauce thickens, about 2 minutes.
Meanwhile, warm the cooked rice in a shallow dish; gently fold in a very small amount of the glaze to give the rice a subtle color and flavor.
Transfer the glazed tofu onto the rice, ensuring a generous spread for maximum crunch.
Sprinkle toasted sesame seeds and sliced green onion over the bowl for garnish.
Drizzle a tiny splash of toasted sesame oil for extra aroma and serve immediately.
Serve the bowl hot, allowing diners to stir the flavors together as they eat.
